body_txt: Sometimes, one line can change everything — for the characters as well as the audience. So, often, if that line has to come up early on, it will be cancelled out by some other noise, such as a truck passing or a plane taking off, or, in the case of dream worlds and other special cases, for no obvious reason whatsoever. Almost universally, this becomes a point for a flashback later in the series, either once the characters (or audience) have learned the rest of the line, or once the conditions have been met for everyone to be clued in as to what happened. Clearing up the issue of what was said usually qualifies as The Reveal (or, if the author hates you, The Un-Reveal ). Compare to: Jackhammered Conversation , Plot-Based Photograph Obfuscation , Instant Mystery, Just Delete Scene , Conveniently Interrupted Document . Contrast Exposition Cut , where the audience already knows what's being said and the characters can understand clearly.
